Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Deterministic Precision Allocation via Hessian Diagnostics
Short Hypothesis: Precision allocation in mixed-precision inference can be optimized by leveraging curvature diagnostics (e.g., Hessian conditioning) to deterministically route computations to appropriate precision tiers, achieving superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs compared to heuristic or activation-based methods.
Related Work: Existing mixed-precision frameworks like Spectral Quantization Routing (founder_1) rely on activation geometry but do not explicitly incorporate curvature diagnostics. Our proposal distinguishes itself by using Hessian conditioning to guide precision switching, providing formal guarantees on numerical stability and computational overhead.
Abstract: Mixed-precision inference promises significant efficiency gains but often relies on heuristic or activation-based precision allocation strategies. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a deterministic framework that leverages curvature diagnostics (e.g., Hessian conditioning) to guide precision allocation. CAPR computes Hessian diagnostics on-the-fly using low-overhead spectral estimators, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict computational budgets. We theoretically ground CAPR with PAC-Bayes bounds that link curvature conditioning to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CAPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing heuristic fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, CAPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference.
Experiments: Implement CAPR on a single-GPU setup, integrating Hessian diagnostics via randomized SVD.
Evaluate CAPR on WILDS benchmarks, comparing throughput and accuracy against static precision and activation-based methods (e.g., Spectral Quantization Routing).
Analyze the relationship between Hessian conditioning and precision sensitivity, quantifying the impact of curvature-aware routing on numerical stability.
Measure computational overhead of Hessian diagnostics, ensuring strict adherence to single-GPU constraints.
Validate theoretical PAC-Bayes bounds through empirical generalization error analysis.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Computational overhead of Hessian diagnostics may exceed practical limits, requiring further optimization.
Curvature diagnostics may not generalize well to all architectures or tasks, limiting applicability.
Theoretical bounds may be too conservative, leading to suboptimal precision allocation.
Single-GPU constraints may limit scalability to larger models or datasets.
